Notice Of Motion To Amend The Original Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

5 - Plaintiff Ramona Mayon's Motion to Amend the Original Complaint is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff neither timely filed nor timely served her motion the instant motion to amend. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is outside State of California but within the United States adds 10 calendar days to "any period of notice"]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Plaintiffs filed and served her motion on 1/20/2026, which is not 16 court days before 2/3/2026, the hearing date noticed. Plaintiff's instant motion to amend is off calendar for non-compliance.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT LAWYERS' COMMITTEE FOR CIVIL RIGHTS THE SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights the San Francisco Bay Area's Demurer is OVERRULED AS MOOT.

Given the court's order granting of Defendant Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights the San Francisco Bay Area's companion Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff Ramona Mayon's complaint under the Anti-SLAPP Statute, the court overrules Defendant's demurrer as moot.

Four proposed orders are submitted for the court' s review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
